dances.name: BSA CUT
dances.history: The Botswana Student Association (BSA) at the Central University of Technology (CUT) was formed to unite and support students from Botswana studying in South Africa. It serves as a home away from home, fostering a strong sense of community and preserving Botswana's rich cultural heritage.
dances.cultural_significance: BSA CUT plays a crucial role in maintaining cultural identity among international students. It celebrates the vibrant traditions of Botswana through various events, ensuring that students remain connected to their roots while navigating their academic journeys.
dances.artistic_elements: The association frequently showcases traditional Botswana customs, including vibrant cultural attire, rhythmic poetry, and dynamic folk dances that highlight the country's diverse ethnic groups and celebrations.

dances.name: Famo
dances.history: Famo music and dance originated in the early 20th century among Basotho migrant mineworkers. It started as a form of social commentary and storytelling through music.
dances.cultural_significance: Famo is a vital expression of the working-class experiences and has grown into a powerful cultural identifier for many in the Mangaung region.
dances.artistic_elements: Accompanied by the accordion and oil-can drum, Famo dancing involves energetic, rhythmic stepping and the waving of the lebolobolo (stick). Dancers often adorn themselves in specific blankets indicating their affiliations.

dances.name: Mohobelo
dances.history: Mohobelo is the traditional men's dance of the Basotho, originally a war dance or an expression of masculine strength, often characterized by high kicks and stomping.
dances.cultural_significance: Today, Mohobelo is performed in Mangaung to honor ancestors, celebrate harvests, and instill a sense of pride and discipline among young men in the community.
dances.artistic_elements: The choreography involves coordinated, high-kicking leaps and stomping, accompanied by rhythmic chanting and singing. Dancers often wear Basotho hats (mokorotlo) and traditional blankets.

interviews.name: All members of the DEVCORE
interviews.description: The full DEVCORE team discusses the research process, community engagement, and the technical journey behind documenting Mangaung's cultural dance heritage.
